free_text: Low-energy cross-section measurement of the
10B(alpha,n)13N reaction and its impact on neutron
production in first-generation stars
The Sta. ANA pelletron accelerator at
the University of Notre Dame Nuclear Science
Laboratory was used for experiment.
The target was prepared by
electron-gun sputtering a thin layer of enriched 10B
powder onto a 0.5 mm thick tantalum backing. Initial
thickness of the target was (0.70+-0.03)mu-g/cm2.
Compiled by S.H.
OS. REACTION modified in subs.4-6
Deuterated liquid scintillator detector of
type EJ301D with thickness of 7.6 cm and 5.8 cm
diameter was used for neutron detection. The detector
was placed on a swing arm that was rotated to angular
positions of 0, 30, 60, 90, 110, 135, and 155deg with
respect to the beam direction. At higher incident
energies the detector was placed at a distance 20.9 cm,
at energies <1MeV, detector was placed to 5.5 cm.
Overal systematic uncertainty
Total uncertainty
Data taken from tab. 1 of the reference
